text: The robotic platform is docked on the right side of the patient, who is positioned in the Trendelenburg tilt on the left. The surgeon begins the robotic right colectomy by exposing the ileocecal pedicle. Using a monopolar scissor, the dissection proceeds in a middle to lateral fashion near the superior mesenteric axis, quickly reaching the avascular plane between the right mesentery, duodenum, and head of the pancreas.

The robotic platform is docked on the right side of the patient, who is positioned in the Trendelenburg position with a left tilt. The surgeon begins the robotic right colectomy by exposing the ileocecal pedicle. Dissection is performed in a middle to lateral direction near the superior mesenteric axis using a monopolar scissor. The avascular plane between the right mesentery and the duodenum and the head of the pancreas is quickly reached.

The surgeon dissects, clips, and sections the ileocecal pedicle, followed by the isolation, clipping, and sectioning of the right colic pedicle. The colon is mobilized from lateral to medial and bottom to top, with dissection of the tall fascia extending to the right colic flexure, reaching the previously dissected plane. The demobilization is completed using colorepipedal dissection.
The surgeon dissects the ileocecal pedicle, clips it, and sections it. The dissection proceeds to isolate the right colic pedicle, which is also clipped and sectioned.

Following the pedicle dissection, the surgeon mobilizes the colon from lateral to medial and bottom to top, dissecting the tall fascia up to the right colic flexure to connect with the previously established dissection plane. The demobilization is completed with a colorepipedal dissection.

The surgeon prepares the colonic stumps for anastomosis construction, using a vessel sealer energy device to divide the colonic mesentery. ICG fluorescence is employed to assess vascular viability, ensuring division at the edge of the green zone for optimal blood supply. The colon is divided using an end of wrist with blue charge, followed by division of the ileum using an end of wrist with white charge. An isoperistaltic helicolonic side-to-side anastomosis is constructed using an endogrease with blue charge, and the entrance is closed with a running suture.

The surgeon prepares the colonic stumps for anastomosis construction. The colonic mesentery is divided using a vessel sealer energy device. The vascular viability of the colonic stumps is assessed with ICG fluorescence, and the colon is divided at the edge of the green zone, indicating good vascularization. The same procedure is applied to the ileum. An isoperistaltic ileocolonic side-to-side anastomosis is constructed using an endoscopic stapler with a blue cartridge, and the stapler entrance is closed with a running suture.

The surgical placement is carried out through a fine steel incision. The postoperative period was an eventful with patient discharge of postoperative day four. The histological exam shows a well-differentiated adenocarcinoma PT one and zero.
